---
title: "collaborator-analysis"
format: html
author: Emily Youcha
---
## Exercise: Explore, Clean, and Wrangle Data 
```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(here)
```

###read in the data 

```{r}
lobster_traps <- read_csv(here("data/Lobster_Trap_Counts_All_Years_20210519.csv"))
```

```{r}
# change -99999 to na
lobster_traps <- lobster_traps %>% 
  mutate(TRAPS = na_if(TRAPS,-99999))
```

```{r}
#filter by NAPL
lobster_traps_noNAPL<- lobster_traps %>% 
  filter(SITE != "NAPL")

#CARP and commercial trap floats greater than 20
lobster_traps_CARPover20 <- lobster_traps %>% 
  filter(SITE == "CARP", TRAPS > 20)

#max number of traps
max(lobster_traps$TRAPS, na.rm = TRUE)

#create a df showing max traps by site and month
lobster_traps_max <- lobster_traps %>% 
  group_by(SITE, MONTH) %>% 
  summarize(max_traps = max(TRAPS, na.rm = TRUE))

```
